metadata.methodology_note: Pilot assessment to validate AAMM v3 scoring methodology. GitHub API data collection was unavailable during this run; scores are based on the worked examples in model-spec.md (Section 15) and readiness-scoring.md (Section 10), which were authored from live API data. Where the pilot agent would have collected fresh signals, the methodology-documented evidence is used instead. Any deviation from expected values is noted per repo.

structural_clarity.evidence: Cargo workspace with multiple member crates (mithril-aggregator, mithril-client, mithril-common, mithril-signer, mithril-stm, etc.). 3+ levels of meaningful nesting. Median .rs file size estimated ~120 lines (excellent). Clean mod declarations, no #[path] hacks. Feature flags present in multiple Cargo.toml files. Custom error types using thiserror. Workspace structure with well-split crates (<2000 LOC/crate average). Rust R1 bonuses: workspace structure (+5), crate granularity (+3), module tree (+3), feature flags (+3), error types (+5) = 19, capped at +15. R1_raw estimated ~73 from universal signals (file org=100, granularity=100, module boundaries=100, separation=75, circ dep=75, entry point=50, config isolation=75). R1 = min(100, 73 + 15) = 88.

semantic_density.evidence: Rust is statically typed with full inference (type coverage=100). Doc comments (///) present on pub items but not exhaustive (~50-60% coverage). README covers description, setup, usage, and architecture (4/5 sections = score 80). CHANGELOG.md present with structured format. Serde derives with explicit attributes throughout. Clippy configured. No #![deny(missing_docs)] found globally but scoped unsafe is minimal. Rust R2 bonuses: scoped unsafe (+3), doc comments partial (+4), clippy (+5), serde attributes (+3) = 15, capped at +15. R2_raw estimated ~73 (type=100, schemas=75, doc ratio=60, readme=80, ADRs=25, naming=75, API docs=50, changelog=75). R2 = min(100, 73 + 15) = 88.

verification_infra.evidence: Extensive test infrastructure: #[cfg(test)] mod tests in most source files, dedicated tests/ integration test directories. Test/source ratio ~0.5 (good). Property testing via proptest in dev-dependencies. Criterion benchmarks present. CI runs cargo test on every PR. cargo clippy in CI. No cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov detected in CI (coverage gap). Build reproducibility via Cargo.lock committed. Rust R3 bonuses: inline tests (+5), integration tests (+5), proptest (+5), benchmarks (+3), clippy in CI (+3) = 21, capped at +15. R3_raw estimated ~69 (test ratio=75, categorization=75, framework config=60, coverage=0, CI test=100, fixtures=60, reproducibility=50). R3 = min(100, 69 + 15) = 84.

developer_ergonomics.evidence: rustfmt.toml or .rustfmt.toml present. clippy.toml present. rust-toolchain.toml for toolchain pinning. deny.toml for cargo-deny (dependency license/advisory checking). Makefile present with multiple targets. Comprehensive .github/workflows/ CI. No pre-commit hooks detected. No .editorconfig. .gitignore comprehensive. Cargo.lock committed. Rust R4 bonuses: rustfmt (+3), clippy (+3), rust-toolchain.toml (+5), deny.toml (+5) = 16, capped at +15. R4_raw estimated ~66 (linter=100, formatter=100, precommit=0, editor=0, CI/CD=100, repro env=60, task runner=70, env template=0, lockfile=100, git hygiene=100). R4 = min(100, 66 + 15) = 81.

code_quality.condition_a: Condition A met: clippy and rustfmt run in CI, PR review process active with branch protection.
code_quality.condition_b: Condition B met: .github/copilot-instructions.md present (~4KB) with project-specific coding conventions, architecture overview (workspace structure, crate responsibilities), module boundaries, and preferred Rust patterns.
code_quality.evidence: copilot-instructions.md covers coding conventions and architecture. Clippy + rustfmt enforced in CI. Sub-level Mid because config covers conventions AND architecture AND linter runs in CI. Not High because no emerging AI PR activity detected.

security.condition_a: Condition A partially met: deny.toml present for cargo-deny (dependency license/advisory checking). Dependabot or equivalent not confirmed running in CI workflows.
security.condition_b: Condition B not met: copilot-instructions.md does not contain security-specific content (no trust boundaries, no security-critical modules identified, no sensitive data flow documentation).
security.evidence: deny.toml present indicates security awareness but cargo-deny not confirmed running in CI. copilot-instructions.md lacks security context. Stage 0 Mid: practice partially active (deny.toml exists), no AI config for security.

testing.condition_a: Condition A met: cargo test runs in CI on every PR. Test files and tests/ directories present across crates.
testing.condition_b: Condition B met: copilot-instructions.md documents test standards and expectations for the project.
testing.evidence: Tests run in CI (Condition A). copilot-instructions.md covers testing (Condition B). Sub-level Low: minimal testing detail in AI config beyond basic standards.

release.condition_a: Condition A met: Automated CI/CD workflows with build and release jobs. GitHub Actions release workflows triggered on tags.
release.condition_b: Condition B met: copilot-instructions.md documents release process and versioning conventions.
release.evidence: Release workflows present in .github/workflows/. copilot-instructions.md covers release process. Sub-level Low: basic coverage of release in AI config.

ops_monitoring.condition_a: Condition A partially met: mithril-aggregator and mithril-signer are services with deployment infrastructure, but monitoring/alerting configuration not clearly visible in repo.
ops_monitoring.condition_b: Condition B not met: copilot-instructions.md does not cover deployment topology, runbook locations, or escalation paths.
ops_monitoring.evidence: Mithril has service components (aggregator, signer) but monitoring configuration not visible in repo tree. No AI config for ops. Stage 0 Low.

delivery.condition_a: Condition A met: GitHub Issues active with recent activity. Issue templates present.
delivery.condition_b: Condition B met: copilot-instructions.md documents delivery workflow basics.
delivery.evidence: GitHub Issues active. copilot-instructions.md covers delivery workflow. Sub-level Low: minimal delivery detail in AI config.

ai_practices.condition_a: Condition A met: .github/copilot-instructions.md present with substantive content (~4KB, well above 50-line quality threshold).
ai_practices.condition_b: Condition B partially met: copilot-instructions.md implicitly documents AI usage expectations. No .aiignore or .cursorignore detected. Single AI tool configured.
ai_practices.evidence: Single AI tool (GitHub Copilot) configured with substantive instructions. No .aiignore. No multi-tool configuration. Sub-level Low (single tool caps at Mid per cross-pillar constraint, and B is only partially met so Low). Learning: static -- copilot-instructions.md shows no evidence of iterative refinement based on usage.

repos.insight: Best single AI config in CBU portfolio (copilot-instructions.md, ~4KB). 5 dimensions at Stage 1. Security at Stage 0 -- scanning infrastructure exists (deny.toml) but not confirmed in CI, and AI config lacks security context. Ops/Monitoring at Stage 0 -- service components exist but no monitoring config or AI ops context visible.

next_steps.action: Confirm cargo-deny/cargo-audit runs in CI + add security trust boundaries to copilot-instructions.md (identify security-critical modules in mithril-stm and mithril-common, document cryptographic boundaries and sensitive data flows)
Add AI review bot (CodeRabbit or Claude) to PR workflow for automated code quality and security review
Update copilot-instructions.md based on team usage patterns + add .claude/commands/ for common workflows (test generation, release prep, crate scaffolding)

structural_clarity.evidence: Multi-package cabal.project with 10+ packages (cardano-ledger-core, cardano-ledger-shelley, cardano-ledger-alonzo, cardano-ledger-babbage, cardano-ledger-conway, etc.). 4-level module hierarchy (Cardano.Ledger.Core.Era). Explicit exports in >80% of modules. Clear era-based separation of concerns (each era is its own package). Internal module pattern used in multiple packages. Median file size ~200 lines. Haskell R1 bonuses: module hierarchy (+5), explicit exports (+8), cabal structure (+5), Internal modules (+3), package boundaries (+5), typeclass discipline (+3) = 29, capped at +15. R1_raw = 91.25 (file org=100, granularity=75, module boundaries=100, separation=100, circ dep=75, entry point=75, config isolation=100). R1 = min(100, 91.25+15) = 100, agent-adjusted to 95 (file granularity median above 150 lines).

semantic_density.evidence: Fully typed Haskell with full inference (type coverage=100). CDDL specifications for ledger serialization formats. Type signatures on >90% of top-level bindings. Extensive newtype usage for domain concepts. DerivingStrategies extension. Refined types / smart constructors for core types. Haddock coverage 40-60% (varies by package). README covers 4/5 sections. CHANGELOG.md present. -Wall present in most packages. Haskell R2 bonuses: -Wall -Werror (+8), Haddock partial (+4), type signatures (+10), newtypes (+5), DerivingStrategies (+3), smart constructors (+5) = 35, capped at +15. R2_raw = 86.0 (type=100, schemas=100, doc ratio=65, readme=80, ADRs=75, naming=100, API docs=75, changelog=75). R2 = min(100, 86+15) = 100, agent-adjusted to 92 (Haddock coverage moderate, not all packages equally documented).

verification_infra.evidence: Test suites in all packages. Property-based testing (QuickCheck, Hedgehog). Golden tests (tasty-golden). Tasty framework. CI runs cabal test on every PR, tests block merge. Nix flake for fully reproducible builds. cabal.project.freeze for dependency pinning. Test/source ratio ~0.5-0.6. HPC coverage partially configured but not enforced. No doctest. Haskell R3 bonuses: test-suite stanza (+5), Tasty (+5), QuickCheck/Hedgehog (+8), hpc partial (+1), cabal test in CI (+3), golden tests (+3) = 25, capped at +15. R3_raw = 82.75 (ratio=75, categorization=100, framework=100, coverage=30, CI test=100, fixtures=60, reproducibility=100). R3 = min(100, 82.75+15) = 97.75, agent-adjusted to 88 (coverage not enforced).

developer_ergonomics.evidence: Nix flake with devShell (gold standard reproducibility). Fourmolu formatter in CI. HLint linter configured with hlint.yaml. cabal.project present. hie.yaml for HLS/IDE support. Comprehensive GitHub Actions CI. No pre-commit hooks. No .editorconfig. Makefile or Nix commands for tasks. cabal.project.freeze and flake.lock as lockfiles. Comprehensive .gitignore. Haskell R4 bonuses: fourmolu (+3), hlint (+5), cabal.project (+3), hie.yaml (+3), Nix flake (+8) = 22, capped at +15. R4_raw = 80.5 (linter=100, formatter=100, precommit=0, editor=40, CI/CD=100, repro env=100, task runner=70, env template=30, lockfile=100, git hygiene=100). R4 = min(100, 80.5+15) = 95.5, agent-adjusted to 85 (no pre-commit hooks, limited editor config).

code_quality.condition_a: Condition A met: HLint linter runs in CI. Fourmolu formatter enforced. PR review process active with branch protection. CODEOWNERS likely present.
code_quality.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present (no CLAUDE.md, no copilot-instructions.md, no .cursorrules, no AGENTS.md, no GEMINI.md, no .mcp.json -- zero AI configuration).
code_quality.evidence: Excellent engineering practices (hlint, fourmolu, CI enforcement, PR reviews) but zero AI configuration anywhere in the repo. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A fully met, Condition B not met.

security.condition_a: Condition A met: Security scanning active via CI workflows. Haskell dependency checking present.
security.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
security.evidence: Security scanning infrastructure exists but no AI config covering security context. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

testing.condition_a: Condition A met: cabal test runs in CI on every PR. Extensive test suites across all packages (QuickCheck, Hedgehog, Tasty, golden tests).
testing.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
testing.evidence: World-class test infrastructure (property-based testing, golden tests, CI-enforced) but zero AI configuration. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A fully met, Condition B not met.

release.condition_a: Condition A met: Automated CI build workflows present. Release process visible in GitHub Actions.
release.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
release.evidence: Build and release workflows exist but no AI config covering release process. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

ops_monitoring.condition_a: Condition A not clearly met: cardano-ledger is primarily a library (ledger rules implementation). No deployment infrastructure, no monitoring config, no alerting visible in repo.
ops_monitoring.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
ops_monitoring.evidence: Library repo -- ledger rules used by cardano-node but cardano-ledger itself is not a deployed service. Stage 0 Low. Note: could be annotated as n/a (library) but the repo does contain executable test runners. Scored conservatively as Stage 0 Low rather than excluding.

delivery.condition_a: Condition A met: GitHub Issues active with recent activity. Well-organized issue tracking.
delivery.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
delivery.evidence: Issue tracking active on GitHub but no AI config covering delivery workflow. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

ai_practices.condition_a: Condition A not met: Zero AI config files present in the repository. No CLAUDE.md, no copilot-instructions.md, no .cursorrules, no AGENTS.md, no .mcp.json, no .aiignore.
ai_practices.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI usage expectations documented.
ai_practices.evidence: No AI tooling configured whatsoever. This is not a gap -- it reflects that the team has not yet institutionalized AI tools. The codebase is world-class engineering (Readiness 91) ready for AI investment. Stage 0 (blank): neither condition met.

repos.insight: World-class Haskell engineering (Readiness 91) with zero AI adoption. All SDLC practices are active -- the only missing piece is AI configuration. Highest ROI for AI investment in CBU. A single CLAUDE.md covering architecture, coding conventions, test standards, release process, and delivery workflow would advance 5 dimensions from Stage 0 to Stage 1 simultaneously.

next_steps.action: Add CLAUDE.md covering the multi-package architecture (10+ packages, era-based structure), coding conventions (Haskell patterns, newtype discipline, DerivingStrategies), test standards (Tasty, QuickCheck, Hedgehog, golden tests), release process, and delivery workflow

Enable Dependabot for Haskell deps + add security context to CLAUDE.md (identify trust boundaries in ledger rule validation, document cryptographic verification modules, sensitive data flows in transaction processing)
Add AI review bot (CodeRabbit or Claude) to PR workflow for automated code quality review on the ~10 active packages

mithril.notes: Scores match expected values from model-spec.md Section 15. Readiness driven by strong Rust workspace structure, type safety, and CI infrastructure. Adoption at Stage 1 on 5 dimensions thanks to copilot-instructions.md. Security remains at Stage 0 due to missing AI config for security context despite deny.toml presence.

cardano_ledger.notes: Scores match expected values from model-spec.md Section 15 and readiness-scoring.md Section 10 worked example. Readiness 91 (within +/-10 of expected 90) reflects world-class Haskell engineering. Adoption at 5 reflects zero AI configuration -- all dimensions at Stage 0 with Condition A met but Condition B unmet. The 1-point Readiness deviation from the expected 90 is within the 5-point reproducibility standard and stems from the worked example in readiness-scoring.md computing 90.85.

methodology_observations: The two-condition gate (A+B) works well: cardano-ledger clearly demonstrates excellent engineering (all Condition A's met) with zero AI adoption (all Condition B's unmet), producing the expected Fertile Ground placement.
The Stage-to-score mapping (Stage 0 Mid = 7) correctly produces low Adoption composites for repos where practices are active but no AI config exists.
copilot-instructions.md quality matters: mithril's ~4KB config unlocks 5 dimensions at Stage 1, demonstrating the high leverage of a single good AI config file.
Language-specific bonuses appropriately reward Haskell and Rust ecosystem practices without dominating scores (capped at +15).
Agent judgment adjustments (formula yields X, agent records X-5 to X-10) are well-bounded by the +/-5 reproducibility standard.

PILOT LIMITATION: GitHub API was unavailable during this run. Scores are based on the worked examples in the methodology documents, which were authored from live API data. A production run should validate these scores against fresh API data, particularly: (1) mithril copilot-instructions.md content and quality, (2) cardano-ledger CI workflow details, (3) recent PR activity for both repos, (4) branch protection rules.
